overview.coreObjective: Build a unified inventory and order management platform that lets Meridian Retail Group track stock across multiple warehouses in real time, automate reordering, and give store staff and customers a consistent view of product availability across online and in-store channels.

flows.title: Stock Reorder Flow
steps: System detects a SKU has fallen below its configured reorder threshold
System generates a draft purchase order for the supplier linked to that SKU
Inventory Manager reviews and approves or edits the draft purchase order
Approved purchase order is sent to the supplier via email
Warehouse Operator receives incoming stock and scans items against the purchase order
System updates on-hand inventory counts once receiving is confirmed

flows.title: Online Order Fulfillment Flow
steps: Customer places an order on the storefront
System checks real-time stock availability across all warehouses
System allocates the order to the nearest warehouse with sufficient stock
Warehouse Operator picks and packs the order
System generates a shipping label and notifies the customer
Customer tracks shipment status until delivery

journeys.title: Customer — Check Product Availability In-Store
journeys.goal: Find out whether a product is available at a nearby physical store before visiting
steps: Searches for a product on the storefront
Enters or selects a nearby store location
Views real-time stock count for that store

functionalRequirements.title: Staff Login
functionalRequirements.description: Store staff, warehouse operators, inventory managers, and administrators can log in with a company email and password.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid credentials show an inline error without revealing which field was wrong
Account locks for 15 minutes after 5 failed login attempts
Session expires after 8 hours of inactivity

functionalRequirements.title: Role-Based Access Control
functionalRequirements.description: Each staff account is assigned exactly one role (Store Staff, Warehouse Operator, Inventory Manager, Administrator) that determines which screens and actions are available.
acceptanceCriteria: Warehouse Operators cannot access purchase order approval screens
Only Administrators can create or modify staff accounts

functionalRequirements.title: Real-Time Stock Lookup
functionalRequirements.description: Any authenticated user can view current stock counts for a given SKU, broken down by warehouse and store location.
acceptanceCriteria: Stock counts reflect changes within 5 seconds of a scan or sale
Out-of-stock locations are visually distinguished from low-stock locations

functionalRequirements.title: Automated Reorder Threshold
functionalRequirements.description: Inventory Managers can configure a minimum stock threshold per SKU per warehouse, below which the system automatically drafts a purchase order.
acceptanceCriteria: Thresholds can be set individually per warehouse, not just globally per SKU
Draft purchase orders appear in a pending-approval queue, never sent automatically without review

functionalRequirements.title: Order Placement
functionalRequirements.description: Customers can add products to a cart and place an order, with stock availability validated at checkout.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkout blocks submission if an item goes out of stock while in the cart
Customer receives an order confirmation email within 1 minute of purchase

functionalRequirements.title: Warehouse Allocation
functionalRequirements.description: The system automatically allocates each order to the nearest warehouse with sufficient stock to fulfill it.
acceptanceCriteria: If no single warehouse can fulfill the full order, the system flags it for manual split-shipment review

functionalRequirements.title: Barcode Scanning for Receiving
functionalRequirements.description: Warehouse Operators can scan incoming shipment barcodes via the mobile app to reconcile received stock against an open purchase order.
acceptanceCriteria: Scanning an item not on the purchase order prompts a discrepancy flag
Partial receipt of a purchase order is supported and tracked

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Stock Data Consistency
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: Stock counts shown to customers on the storefront must never overstate actual availability by more than a 30-second staleness window, to avoid overselling.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: Mobile Scanner Offline Tolerance
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The warehouse mobile scanner app must queue scans locally and sync automatically if warehouse WiFi connectivity drops, without losing scan data.

nonFunctionalRequirements.title: API Response Time
nonFunctionalRequirements.description: 95th percentile response time for stock lookup and checkout endpoints must be under 300ms under normal load.

constraints: Must integrate with Meridian's existing SAP-based supplier ordering system
Warehouse mobile app must run on existing Zebra handheld scanner hardware already deployed in warehouses
Must support at least 4 physical warehouses and 25 retail store locations at launch
assumptions: Meridian will provide SAP API credentials and sandbox access by the start of integration work
Existing product catalog data (SKUs, descriptions, images) will be provided as a one-time CSV export for initial import
Store-level point-of-sale systems are out of scope for real-time integration in this phase and will report stock via nightly batch sync
outOfScope: Native mobile app for customers (responsive web storefront only for MVP)

glossary.definition: Stock Keeping Unit — a unique identifier for a distinct product/variant
The minimum stock level at which a SKU automatically triggers a draft purchase order
An order fulfilled from more than one warehouse because no single warehouse holds full stock
